api: Workday tenant REST API (real, distinct product from the SOAP-based Workday Web Services this bundle's PRIOR docs_url pointed at; the real REST API's own directory is https://community.workday.com/sites/default/files/file-hosting/restapi/)
scope: BLOCKED (DOCS_UNREACHABLE) full-surface expansion attempt: this bundle's original metadata.json docs_url (https://community.workday.com/sites/default/files/file-hosting/productionapi/index.html) resolves to the Workday Web Services (WWS) SOAP-service directory, v46.1 -- a DIFFERENT product from the REST `ccx/api/v1` interface this bundle's streams and the legacy connector actually call; that mismatch has been corrected here (see metadata.json). The REAL REST API directory (linked above) is a JavaScript SPA whose per-service OpenAPI 2.0 JSON specs (e.g. staffing_v1, common_v1 -- the services that would document workers/organizations/positions in full) are fetched by client-side code from a path that returns 404 unauthenticated, and the equivalent community.workday.com/rest/reference landing page requires HTTP Basic auth (verified live: 401 with `WWW-Authenticate: Basic realm="Sling (Development)"`) gated behind a Workday customer/partner account. No public, unauthenticated OpenAPI spec or endpoint reference exists for Workday's REST API; the vendor's own docs are reachable only to logged-in tenant/partner accounts. Per docs/migration/conventions.md §6, this is a DOCS_UNREACHABLE blocker: the connector's real documentation is dead/paywalled to this migration agent and no alternative source of truth (a public spec, or Workday-published sample responses) exists at the level of detail needed to respectably add new streams/writes without guessing field names or endpoint paths. The 3 existing legacy-parity streams (workers, organizations, positions) are RE-VERIFIED as accurate against the only available source of truth -- the hand-written legacy connector's own request/response handling (internal/connectors/workday/workday.go) -- and are unchanged. No new streams or writes were added; inventing endpoint paths or field shapes without a verifiable source would violate the 'REAL closed-vocabulary category+specific reason' requirement this pass demands. See docs.md Known limits for the full blocker writeup.

excluded.reason: single-worker detail; would duplicate the workers list stream's per-item object shape (unverifiable in full without the gated REST API spec, but the list/detail relationship itself is a standard, well-established Workday REST API convention)

worker mutation on a live HR/payroll system of record requires a change/business-process context (Workday's staffing business processes) this dialect cannot express as a plain data write, and the real request/response shape cannot be verified -- Workday's own REST API reference is DOCS_UNREACHABLE (gated behind customer/partner login; see api_surface.json scope note and docs.md Known limits)
